

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)

# --- 1. Filter Data for Each Drug (Strict Logic) ---

# A. Hydroxychloroquine (Standard)
hcq_data <- pharma_table1 %>%
  filter(drug_std %in% c("hydroxychloroquine", "chloroquine")) %>%
  mutate(plot_group = "Hydroxychloroquine")

# B. Ivermectin (Standard)
ivm_data <- pharma_table1 %>%
  filter(drug_std == "ivermectin") %>%
  mutate(plot_group = "Ivermectin")

# C. Convalescent Plasma (Use Class/Label)
cp_data <- pharma_table1 %>%
  filter(drug_class == "Convalescent plasma" | label == "Immunotherapy (Conv. Plasma)") %>%
  mutate(plot_group = "Convalescent Plasma")

# D. Lopinavir/Ritonavir (Combo Check)
# We need to find arms that have BOTH.
# Step 1: Identify Arm IDs that have Lopinavir
lop_arms <- pharma_table1 %>% 
  filter(drug_std == "lopinavir") %>% 
  select(st_reg_id, arm_id)

# Step 2: Identify Arm IDs that have Ritonavir
rit_arms <- pharma_table1 %>% 
  filter(drug_std == "ritonavir") %>% 
  select(st_reg_id, arm_id)

# Step 3: Keep only the INTERSECTION (Arms with both)
combo_arms <- inner_join(lop_arms, rit_arms, by = c("st_reg_id", "arm_id"))

# Step 4: Filter the main table for these validated combo arms
lpv_data <- pharma_table1 %>%
  semi_join(combo_arms, by = c("st_reg_id", "arm_id")) %>%
  # Just keep one row per arm to avoid double plotting (since 2 rows exist per combo)
  distinct(st_reg_id, arm_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(plot_group = "Lopinavir/Ritonavir")


# --- 2. Combine & Clean for Plotting ---
all_target_data <- bind_rows(hcq_data, ivm_data, cp_data, lpv_data) %>%
  filter(st_reg_date >= "2020-01-01", st_reg_date <= "2025-12-31") %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(st_reg_date),
    phase_simple = case_when(
      str_detect(st_phase, "phase 3|phase 4") ~ "Phase 3/4 (Confirmatory)",
      str_detect(st_phase, "phase 1|phase 2") ~ "Phase 1/2 (Exploratory)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(phase_simple != "Other")

# --- 3. Define Stop Dates ---
stop_lines <- tibble(
  plot_group = c("Hydroxychloroquine", "Ivermectin", "Lopinavir/Ritonavir", "Convalescent Plasma"),
  stop_date = as.Date(c("2020-07-04", "2021-03-31", "2020-07-04", "2021-12-07")),
  label = c("WHO Stop (July '20)", "WHO Stop (Mar '21)", "WHO Stop (July '20)", "WHO Stop (Dec '21)")
)

# --- 4. Plot Function (To keep it clean) ---
plot_redundancy <- function(data_subset, group_name) {
  
  stop_info <- stop_lines %>% filter(plot_group == group_name)
  
  p <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = st_reg_date, y = phase_simple, color = phase_simple)) +
    
    # Jittered Points
    geom_jitter(height = 0.2, width = 0, size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Stop Line
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(stop_info$stop_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
    annotate("text", x = stop_info$stop_date + 20, y = 2.4, 
             label = stop_info$label, hjust = 0, color = "red", fontface = "bold") +
    
    # Formatting
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y", limits = as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2025-12-31"))) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Phase 1/2 (Exploratory)" = "#4575b4", "Phase 3/4 (Confirmatory)" = "#d73027")) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Redundant trial?:", group_name),
      #subtitle = "Blue dots (Exploratory) after Red Line = Research Waste",
      x = NULL, y = NULL, color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
  
  print(p)
}

# --- 5. Generate Plots ---
unique_groups <- unique(all_target_data$plot_group)

for(g in unique_groups) {
  plot_redundancy(all_target_data %>% filter(plot_group == g), g)
}

```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)

# --- 1. Define Stop Dates ---
# WHO/NIH Guidelines generally recommended against routine use outside trials by mid-2021
# (e.g., NIH Guidelines: Feb 2021 for Zinc/Vit C in non-deficient patients)
stop_lines_supplements <- tibble(
  plot_group = c("Zinc", "Vitamin C"),
  stop_date = as.Date(c("2021-02-11", "2021-02-11")), 
  label = c("NIH Guidelines: Against (Feb '21)", "NIH Guidelines: Against (Feb '21)")
)

# --- 2. Filter Data for Zinc & Vitamin C ---
# We use str_detect to catch "zinc sulfate", "zinc chloride", etc.
supplements_data <- pharma_table1 %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(str_to_lower(drug_std), "zinc|vitamin c|ascorbic")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(st_reg_date),
    phase_simple = case_when(
      str_detect(st_phase, "phase 3|phase 4") ~ "Confirmatory (Phase 3/4)",
      str_detect(st_phase, "phase 1|phase 2") ~ "Exploratory (Phase 1/2)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    plot_group = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(drug_std), "zinc") ~ "Zinc",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(drug_std), "vitamin c|ascorbic") ~ "Vitamin C",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(phase_simple != "Other", plot_group != "Other", 
         st_reg_date >= "2020-01-01", st_reg_date <= "2025-12-31")

# --- 3. Plot Function ---
plot_supplement <- function(data_subset, group_name) {
  
  stop_info <- stop_lines_supplements %>% filter(plot_group == group_name)
  
  p <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x = st_reg_date, y = phase_simple, color = phase_simple)) +
    
    # Jittered Points
    geom_jitter(height = 0.2, width = 0, size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
    
    # Stop Line
    geom_vline(xintercept = as.numeric(stop_info$stop_date), linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
    annotate("text", x = stop_info$stop_date + 20, y = 2.4, 
             label = stop_info$label, hjust = 0, color = "red", fontface = "bold") +
    
    # Formatting
    scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 year", date_labels = "%Y", limits = as.Date(c("2020-01-01", "2025-12-31"))) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Exploratory (Phase 1/2)" = "#4575b4", "Confirmatory (Phase 3/4)" = "#d73027")) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Redundancy Check:", group_name),
      subtitle = "Persistence of exploratory trials (Blue) after negative guidelines suggests waste.",
      x = NULL, y = NULL, color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(legend.position = "top", panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())
  
  print(p)
}

# --- 4. Generate Plots ---
unique_groups_supp <- unique(supplements_data$plot_group)

for(g in unique_groups_supp) {
  plot_supplement(supplements_data %>% filter(plot_group == g), g)
}

```
